rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KWAI MUI LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural issues with petition: 1) The petition is incomplete. Page 4 is missing entirely, Item 8 is not included, and the petition is not signed by petitioner or his attorney. 2)If a will names a trust or trustee as a devisee, the trustee must be clearly identified in item 8. Where the personal representative and the sole trustee are the same person, or if there is no trustee, the beneficiaries of the trust must be clearly identified. (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.C.2). 3) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 4) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LAMBERT GENZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues (see tentative ruling for 10/08/2024 hearing) that remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 4) Has the petitioner given notice of administration to all known or reasonably ascertainable creditors of the decedent, including public entities? 5) All the information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1) must be provided. Missing information includes whether there are are assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF EILEEN L WALSH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The accounting does not balance, in that total credits exceed total charges by $1.70. The petitioner added $1.70 as a "bank deposit adjustment" receipt to the total charges to make the accounting balance. 2) The conservatee communicated her wishes to terminate the conservatorship. The Court is to determines whether Probate Code section 1861.5 applies to require a hearing to be set for the termination. 3) No petition for substituted judgment has been filed since the scope of representation by counsel for the conservatee was expanded to include estate planning. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JEWEL DENT REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED 8/30/17
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Cancellation Of Deed Under Civil Code 3412, For Relief Under Probate Code 850 Et Seq., And For Damages Under Probate Code 859
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court may determine: 1) Is the TOD Deed in question void on its face, as the TOD Deed failed to identify any beneficiary required by Probate Code section 5622? 2) Is the TOD Deed "an instrument, the invalidity of which is apparent on its face, not to be deemed capable of causing injury" under Civil Code section 3413? 3) Is the Affidavit of Death in question valid in part and void in part? To the extent the Affidavit was intended to be an affidavit of death within the meaning of section 210(a), such an affidavit can be recorded by "any person", presumably including the respondent, to establish the death of a person holding title to real property. To the extent it was intended to be a section 13200 affidavit re real property of small value, however, such an affidavit must be on the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-305 filed with the Court. The Affidavit recorded by the respondent was not on the DE-305 form, nor was it a copy of the DE-305 form certified by the Court. 4) Would the Affidavit, if construed as a section 210(a) affidavit of death of a real property owner, cause serious injury required by Civil Code section 3412 to warrant cancellation? 5) Is the Affidavit, if construed as a section 13200 small real property affidavit, an instrument, the invalidity of which is apparent on its face, not deemed capable of causing injury under Civil Code section 3413? 6) Should the proposed order purporting to compel the respondent to sign a quitclaim deed for each trust real property be accepted? It should be noted that: i) The relief was not included in the petition; and ii) The TOD Deed and Affidavit, if void, did not pass title to either property to the respondent. 7) Should the issue of double damages and attorney fees against the respondent under section 859 be bifurcated? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUSSELL J. BACOSA
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/5/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) If an estate is not ready to close within the statutory time set forth in Probate Code Sect. 12200, the report of status of administration must set forth the status of the estate, including, but not limited to, allegations regarding notice pursuant to Probate Code Sect. 9202, disclosure of all creditor claims and their dispositions, requests for special notice, and any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution. [LRSF 14.35 J.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/22/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROSEMARY BROWN LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate The Rosemary Brown Living Trust Dated June 3, 2023
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUDITH MARY WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/22/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] When did the decedent's parents die? (3) Notwithstanding the waivers of bond, a proposed non-resident personal representative must file a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] Please provide a courtesy copy of (1) the supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, addressing the issues with the petition and (2) the proof of service of notice of the 1/22/25 hearing, showing that a copy of the remote appearance instructions was attached to it to the assigned examiner by 1/8/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 3h(2) of the petition was incomplete.)
